title: AI Agents: Digital Helpers That Plan
summary: An introduction to agentic AI systems that can plan, call tools, and check their own work.

title: Transformers and Attention with a Classroom Analogy
summary: Explains how attention lets a model focus on the most important words or image regions.

summary: Shows how rewards, actions, and policies help AI improve through trial and feedback.

title: AI Safety and Alignment Basics
summary: Introduces why AI systems need guardrails, evaluation, transparency, and human oversight.

summary: Explains models that combine text, images, audio, and video to understand the world.

title: TinyML and Edge AI on Small Devices
summary: Describes how AI can run on phones, sensors, and low-power devices.

title: RAG: Teaching an AI to Use a Library
summary: Explains retrieval-augmented generation using a library and note-card analogy.

summary: Introduces image generation by gradually removing noise until a picture appears.

title: Prompt Engineering and AI Evaluation
summary: Shows how prompts guide AI systems and why outputs should be tested carefully.

title: Robotics Plus AI: From Simulation to the Real World
summary: Connects robotics, simulation, sensors, and AI decision making.

title: AI in Medicine: Useful but Careful
summary: Explains medical AI with a focus on decision support, data bias, privacy, and validation.

title: Open-Source AI Models and Fine-Tuning
summary: Introduces open-source models, fine-tuning, and why reproducibility matters.
